title: Emergency Governance Rollback Policy
summary: Defines the dual-authorized, append-only, deterministically replayable semantics for emergency governance rollback. Emergency override remains fail-closed.

invariants.description: Rollback authority remains dual-authorized. A single operator cannot authorize a rollback unilaterally.
invariants.enforcement: assertRollbackAuthorized throws RollbackAuthorizationError if dual-auth is invalid.

invariants.description: Rollback events are replay-visible. The full event chain is retained and replayable.
invariants.enforcement: replayRollbackChain reconstructs final status from the ordered event sequence.

invariants.description: Rollback is append-only. Events are immutable once created; no mutation or deletion.
invariants.enforcement: createRollbackEvent returns Object.freeze() records. No update path exists.

invariants.description: Rollback reconstruction is deterministic. Same inputs always produce same final chain state.
invariants.enforcement: replayRollbackChain sorts by occurredAt and applies a deterministic state machine.

invariants.description: Emergency override remains fail-closed. Frozen domains cannot be rolled back regardless of authorization.
invariants.enforcement: assertDomainNotFrozen throws RollbackDomainFrozenError before any authorization is checked.

frozen_domain_rationale: Domains capital, scaling, ws-3.5, and treasury are constitutionally frozen pending formal activation standards. Rollback of these domains is prohibited to prevent unintended state reconstruction. Treasury was added in UTV2-1179 per PM ruling 2026-05-28.
